subject: Science
chapter: Changes Around Us: Physical and Chemical

activities_projects.title: Stoichiometry of Gas Production
activities_projects.objective: Quantify the gas produced in a chemical change using displacement
steps: Set up a pneumatic trough to collect gas by downward displacement of water
React a measured mass of baking soda with excess vinegar
Collect the $CO_2$ and record the volume in milliliters
Calculate the volume of gas produced per gram of reactant
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies quantitative measurement to verify chemical changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of data collection and volume calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Exothermic vs. Endothermic Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Differentiate changes based on energy transfer
steps: Dissolve urea in water and record the temperature drop (Endothermic)
Add water to quicklime and record the temperature rise (Exothermic)
Plot a time-temperature graph for both reactions
Explain the breaking and forming of bonds in terms of energy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the thermodynamic nature of physical and chemical changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the graph and energy-flow explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reaction Rate and Particle Size
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how physical state affects chemical change speed
steps: Drop a whole tablet into water and time the reaction until the fizzing stops
Crush an identical tablet into fine powder and repeat
Analyze the results based on 'surface area to volume ratio'
Conclude how physical preparation influences chemical kinetics
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the intersection of physical properties and reaction rates
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative data table and logical conclusion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Advanced Crystallization & Seeding
activities_projects.objective: Control crystal geometry through cooling rates
steps: Prepare two identical saturated solutions of Copper Sulphate
Cool one rapidly in an ice bath and the other slowly at room temperature
Examine the size and shape of crystals under a magnifying glass
Explain the relationship between cooling rate and crystal lattice formation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Observes how physical conditions dictate the outcome of a physical change
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed sketch of crystals and explanation of cooling rates (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mass Conservation Verification
activities_projects.objective: Prove the Law of Conservation of Mass in a chemical change
steps: Weigh the flask with vinegar and the balloon with soda together
Trigger the reaction without opening the system (tip the soda into the flask)
Weigh the entire setup again after the reaction finishes
Discuss any minor deviations due to air buoyancy or leaks
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Validates that mass is neither created nor destroyed during chemical changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of mass readings before and after reaction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Catalytic Decomposition of Hydrogen Peroxide
activities_projects.objective: Study the role of a catalyst in a chemical change
materials: Manganese Dioxide or raw potato slices
steps: Observe $H_2O_2$ at room temperature (slow change)
Add the catalyst and observe the rapid evolution of Oxygen
Test the gas with a glowing splinter (it should relight)
Explain why the catalyst itself remains unchanged after the reaction
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies catalysts as facilitators of chemical change
activities_projects.evaluation: Success of the oxygen test and explanation of catalysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Chemistry of Photosynthesis (Simulation)
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the complex chemical change that sustains life
steps: Place Hydrilla in a beaker of water mixed with bicarbonate (source of $CO_2$)
Cover with an inverted funnel and test tube
Count the bubbles produced under different light intensities
Write the balanced chemical equation for the process
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Links biological processes to fundamental chemical changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Data correlating light intensity to reaction rate (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chemical Weathering of Rocks
activities_projects.objective: Simulate the effect of acid rain on different geological materials
steps: Submerge samples in acid for 72 hours
Monitor the pH of the liquid periodically
Examine the rock surfaces for pitting or weight loss
Explain the chemical transformation of Calcium Carbonate to Calcium Sulphate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the impact of chemical changes on the environment
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific report on the vulnerability of different rock types (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Iodine Clock Reaction
steps: Prepare the starch-iodine-peroxide mixture (Solution A and B)
Mix them and time how long it takes for the clear liquid to suddenly turn dark blue
Change the concentration of one reactant and observe the time difference
Explain the 'competing reactions' logic
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explores complex reaction mechanisms and timing
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate timing and concentration-effect analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investigating Corrosion Inhibitors
activities_projects.objective: Experiment with organic inhibitors to slow chemical changes
materials: Green tea extract or Onion juice
steps: Place one nail in salt water (control)
Place another in salt water mixed with green tea extract (tannins act as inhibitors)
Observe the rate of rust formation over a week
Research the molecular mechanism of how inhibitors block active sites
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns advanced methods of controlling unwanted chemical changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Qualitative comparison and research on tannins (10 marks)
